data.title: Regions Financial appoints Roger W. Jenkins to serve on the board
data.description:
• Regions Financial (NYSE:RF) announced Roger W. Jenkins, director and CEO of Murphy Oil Corporation, has been appointed to serve on the board of the company and its subsidiary, Regions Bank.
  • Jenkins’ term on the Regions boards will begin January 1, 2025.
  • With the addition of Jenkins, the Regions boards will consist of 14 members.
  • Upon joining the Regions boards, Jenkins will serve on the Risk and Technology Committees.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple working with Broadcom to develop in-house AI server chips: report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is working with Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) to develop the company's first in-house server chip exclusively designed for artificial intelligence, The Information reported.

The chip, known internally as Baltra, could be ready for mass production by 2026, the news outlet reported, citing people with direct knowledge of the matter. The new AI chip could be used for inferencing, which has the chip capable of processing new data and then hands them off to the large language models to produce an output, a task different than training models, the news outlet added.

The chip will be produced by Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and likely use its 3 nanometer process, The Information added, citing sources.

Broadcom shares rose 3.8% in early trading, while Apple shares rose 0.5%. Nvidia (NVDA), which dominates the AI accelerator market, rose 0.5%.

The plan to bring its own chips to its own servers was born three years ago, but the plan was accelerated after OpenAI's ChatGPT chatbot took the world by storm in December 2022, according to previous reports.

Chief Executive Tim Cook has said previously Apple has some advantages that its competitors in the AI space do not. "We believe in the transformative power and promise of AI, and we believe we have advantages that will differentiate us in this new era, including Apple's unique combination of seamless hardware, software and services integration, groundbreaking Apple's silicon, with our industry-leading neural engines and our unwavering focus on privacy, which underpins everything we create," Cook said on the company's earnings call in May.

Last week, Apple executive Benoit Dupin gave a rare endorsement of Amazon's (AMZN) custom artificial intelligence chips and said the iPhone maker is evaluating Amazon's latest AI chip for pre-training models.

Cupertino, Calif.-based Apple has also used some of Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) chips to train AI models for Apple Intelligence.

The news comes as Apple continued to roll out its Apple Intelligence features on Wednesday. The tech giant released the latest versions of iOS, macOS and iPadOS, all of which include several AI-related features, including the integration of ChatGPT into Siri.

Apple and Broadcom did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: E2open shares fall after Goldman downgrades it to SELL
data.description:
• Shares of E2open Parent Holdings (NYSE:ETWO) opened 8% lower on Wednesday, after Goldman Sachs downgraded it to SELL from NEUTRAL, citing limited opportunity for meaningful growth.
  • The investment firm also cut its price target for E2open Parent (ETWO) to $2.90 from $3.50.
  • Goldman believes ETWO shares are likely to remain under pressure as historically low retention levels, organic sales challenges, and a constrained balance sheet limit the opportunity for the business to return to meaningful growth over the next 12–18 months.
  • “With four consecutive quarters of yoy declines in subscription revenue growth and lowered FY25 guidance, we believe it is prudent to be incrementally cautious around shares of ETWO despite the company trading near trough levels,” Goldman analysts wrote in a research note.
  • Goldman said its updated target multiple reflects slower growth expectations and continued uncertainty around the pace of ETWO’s growth recovery. The target price implies ~7% downside.

data.title: Advanced Micro Devices, Intel see modest increase in notebooks shipped: Citi
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) and Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) experienced a "mild positive" as November notebook shipments increased 2% month over month, according to Citi Research.

Notebook CPUs account for 37% of Intel's sales and 16% of AMD sales, Citi notes. Despite the slight uptick, Citi maintains its fourth quarter notebook forecast of an 8% drop in shipments quarter over quarter due to muted demand.

However, PC unit sales are expected to increase 4% year over year in 2025 compared to the miniscule growth of half a percent in 2024.

Citi maintains a Buy rating on AMD and a Neutral rating on the beleaguered Intel. The financial firm estimates full-year earnings per share of $2.48 for AMD, which is more than the consensus of $2.43. It estimates a loss per share of $0.87 for Intel, versus the consensus calling for a loss per share of $0.84 for the year.

"We believe the in-line November notebook sales are a mild positive for Intel and AMD, as the QTD notebook shipments have achieved 66% of our forecast in 4Q24E," said Citi analyst Christopher Danely, in a note.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: PDCO, GME, GEV, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Wednesday as market participants reacted to the latest CPI inflation data, which matched consensus expectations.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • GameStop (GME) shares climbed over 4% despite mixed FQ3 results, with revenue plunging over 20% Y/Y. The company closed the quarter with $4.62B in cash, significantly higher than $929.2M a year ago. CEO Matt Furlong noted, "We remain focused on right-sizing our cost structure while investing in growth initiatives." The company also stated it does not plan any further at-the-market stock offerings for the current fiscal year.
  • Patterson Companies (PDCO) saw a 34% increase in its stock price following the announcement of a $4.1B acquisition by Patient Square Capital. Under the terms of the agreement, shareholders will receive $31.35 in cash per share, reflecting a 35.7% premium over the company’s closing price on Tuesday. The transaction will be financed through a combination of equity from Patient Square Equity Partners and debt funding from Citi, UBS Investment Bank, and Wells Fargo Bank N.A. The acquisition is expected to close in the fourth quarter of FY2025, after which Patterson will transition to a privately held entity, retaining its headquarters in St. Paul, Minnesota.
  • Stitch Fix (SFIX) shares jumped 24% following a stronger-than-expected FQ1 performance, driven by the exit from its UK business and increased sales per customer, which contributed to a narrower-than-anticipated loss. The positive results have strengthened confidence in the company's growth prospects, with an optimistic outlook for Q2 and FY2025. For Q2, Stitch Fix expects revenue between $290M and $300M, surpassing the consensus estimate of $283.6M, and adjusted EBITDA in the range of $8M to $13M. For FY2025, the company anticipates revenue between $1.14B and $1.18B, aligning with the consensus of $1.14B, and adjusted EBITDA of $25M to $36M, while maintaining stable gross margins of 44% to 45%.
  • Albertsons (ACI) shares rose nearly 4% after the company terminated its $25B merger with Kroger (KR), following a federal judge's ruling to block the deal. The decision was fueled by the FTC’s opposition and regulatory concerns over anti-competitive effects. Albertsons (ACI) also filed a lawsuit against Kroger for breach of contract, seeking damages and a $600M termination fee. Despite the merger collapse, Cerberus Capital Management expressed confidence in Albertsons' strength as an independent entity.

Biggest stock losers

  • GE Vernova (GEV) shares dropped 3% after the company reaffirmed its FY 2024 guidance but narrowed its adjusted EBITDA margin outlook to 5.5%-6.0% from the previous 5%-7%, raising investor concerns despite revenues and free cash flow trending toward the higher end of prior guidance. While the company raised its FY 2025 forecasts for revenues ($36B-$37B), free cash flow ($2B-$2.5B), and adjusted EBITDA margin (high single digits), these updates failed to ease concerns as revenue guidance remained below the $37.3B analyst consensus. The company also declared a $0.25/share quarterly dividend and approved an initial $6B share repurchase authorization.
  • Dave & Buster’s Entertainment (PLAY) shares tumbled 15% following a downbeat FQ3, with a wider-than-expected loss. The company's profitability was impacted by a material fiscal calendar mismatch, adverse weather, remodel construction, and certain "unusual" items. Adding to the pressure, CEO Chris Morris announced his resignation. The board appointed Chairman Kevin Sheehan as interim CEO.
  • Macy's (M) shares slumped as much as 11% despite surpassing FQ3 estimates, as the retailer cut its FY2024 profit outlook to reflect adjustments for misstated delivery expenses, along with related accrual and tax effects. The company now forecasts full-year sales of $22.3B-$22.5B (midpoint $22.4B), slightly higher than the prior range of $22.1B-$22.4B and above the $22.13B consensus. However, adjusted EPS guidance was lowered to $2.25-$2.50 (midpoint $2.375), falling short of the $2.73 consensus and down from the previous range of $2.55-$2.90.

data.title: MetLife, General Atlantic team up to launch a new reinsurance company
data.description:
MetLife (NYSE:MET) and private-market investor General Atlantic have joined forces to form a to-be-registered Class E Bermuda-based life and annuity reinsurance company, the firms said Wednesday, in a bid to enhance returns from low-risk insurance assets.

Due to roll out in the first half of next year, Chariot Reinsurance is anticipated to have an initial combined equity investment of more than $1B. MetLife (NYSE:MET) and General Atlantic will each initially own about 15% of the equity.

The remaining equity interests will be held by third-party stakeholders, including property and casualty insurer Chubb (CB), which is expected to be an anchor third-party investor.

MetLife (MET) said it expects to initially reinsure to Chariot Re a ~$10B block of liabilities composed of structured settlement annuity contracts and group annuity contracts associated with pension risk transfers. Going forward, MetLife Investment Management and General Atlantic will exclusively provide asset management services to Chariot Re.

Cynthia Smith, a 30-year veteran of MetLife (MET), will serve as Chariot Re's CEO. She most recently led MetLife’s Group Benefits Regional Business.

Toby Srihiran Brown, global head of Reinsurance at MetLife, will represent the company on Chariot Re’s board. It is also expected that Graves Tompkins, chief operating officer at General Atlantic, will represent the firm on Chariot Re’s Board of Directors.

(MET) shares edged up 0.5% in premarket trading.

data.title: Google requests FTC to break up Microsoft-OpenAI's cloud deal - report
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with FTC's statement of no comment on the report.

Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) unit Google has requested the U.S. Federal Trade Commission to break up Microsoft's (NASDAQ:MSFT) exclusive deal to host OpenAI's technology on its cloud servers, The Information reported.

The discussions occurred after the U.S. regulator asked Google about Microsoft's business practices as part of a broader probe, the report added, citing a person involved in the talks.

"We have no comment," FTC said in an email to Seeking Alpha.

Microsoft's competitors in the cloud server space, such as Google, also want to host OpenAI's large language models, or LLMs, so that their cloud customers do not need to access Microsoft's servers to get access to the ChatGPT-maker's technology, the report noted.

Companies that buy OpenAI's technology via Microsoft may have to see additional charges if they do not already use Microsoft's servers to carry out their operations, the report added.

Google and other rivals have highlighted that these new costs will impact customers, the report noted.

Microsoft, Google, OpenAI and the FTC did not respond to an email from Seeking Alpha till the time of publication of this report.

data.title: SEI announces acquisition of Boston-based LifeYield
data.description:
• Asset manager SEI (NASDAQ:SEIC) announced on Wednesday the acquisition of the Boston-based tax-smart technology provider, LifeYield.
  • SEI said the acquisition is not a significant acquisition of assets or otherwise material to its operations or financial results.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zeta gets Neutral rating at Goldman as proprietary data benefits are balanced by medium-term risks
data.description:
Goldman Sachs began coverage of Zeta Global (NYSE:ZETA) with a Neutral rating, noting that the company's proprietary data advantages are balanced by medium-term risks.

The firm set a $30 price target on the shares of Zeta — which operates a cloud platform that provides enterprises with consumer intelligence and marketing automation software.

Analysts led by Gabriela Borges said they believe Zeta has one of the more proprietary datasets in the marketing and adtech industry, as expressed by 3K to 5K identifiers on around 240M U.S. adults that the company collects from sources such as Visa credit cards and user reviews at online publishers.

Zeta successfully monetizes this dataset within its customer base of over 450 enterprises via a Customer Data Platform, CDP, layer and marketing tools that allow these customers to achieve more accurate consumer targeting and subsequently lower customer acquisition cost, or CAC, according to the analysts.

The analysts noted that with the stock up over 150% year-to-date, their positive view of Zeta's differentiated positioning today and 40% upside to the price target is offset by the high stock volatility and what they see as several medium term risks.

The analysts' industry conversations indicated that proprietary data may become less useful as more merchants have access to it and as consumer preferences change, creating pressure on Zeta to constantly innovate (both organically and inorganically).

In addition, the analysts said that 2024 was a particularly strong year for ad spend given the Summer Olympics and the Elections (example: they expect elections will boost Zeta’s revenue growth by 6 points in 2024), leading to a difficult year-over-year comparison in 2025.

Borges and her team noted that privacy regulations are becoming more strict and are difficult to predict, creating an overhang over Zeta's business over the long term.

The analysts added that this was particularly seen in November, when Zeta underperformed the Nasdaq by about 30% on the back of industry debate on its data collection practices. While the company has addressed the concerns in several forums, the analysts believe a broader risk of new regulation, which limits data collection, remains.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Wednesday: M, ADBE and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Wednesday rose, after key consumer inflation data came in-line with estimates. Here are some stocks to watch on Wednesday:

  • Shares of Macy's (M) slumped 11% in pre-market trading, after the department store chain delivered a Q3 drop in comparable sales and cut its annual adjusted profit per share guidance. Weakness in Macy's (M) non-First 50 locations as well as its digital channel and cold weather categories impacted overall quarterly net sales. The company also completed an independent investigation that uncovered $151M of hidden cumulative delivery expenses from Q4 2021 to Q3 2024. See how M performed in charts.
  • Adobe (ADBE) stock will be in focus, ahead of the creative software maker's FQ4 2024 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the San Jose, Calif.-based firm to earn $4.67 per share on revenue of $5.54B. Adobe (ADBE) is known for its Photoshop graphics editor and Premier Pro video editor, but investors recently have increasingly focused on its artificial intelligence (AI) initiatives. ADBE has been continuing the rollout of AI updates to its photography services, and in October introduced over 100 new creative cloud features.
  • Class A shares of Mondelēz (MDLZ) gained nearly 5% ahead of market open, after the confectionary and snacks manufacturer authorized a new stock buyback of up to $9B. Mondelēz (MDLZ) - the owner of popular brands like Cadbury, Oreo and Toblerone - said the new stock repurchase would be effective January 1, 2025. "Given current market conditions, share repurchase remains an opportunity and key priority. The company remains committed to an acquisition strategy that is focused on bolt-on assets similar to recent acquisitions of Chipita, Clif and Ricolino," MDLZ said.
  • General Motors (GM) stock climbed nearly 2% in pre-market trading. The legacy U.S. automaker on Tuesday said it would realign its autonomous driving strategy and prioritize development of advanced driver assistance systems. As part of the plan, GM (GM) will no longer fund its Cruise unit's development of a robotaxi. The announcement by the company has major implications for other robotaxi developers such as Uber (UBER), Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet's (GOOG)(GOOGL) Waymo.
